item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the intense buildup led to an anticlimactic reveal (vomiting up a tiny animatronic clone and…then dying), but I love the idea that Disney is bigger than the Foundation, and also has multiple anomalies they are directly connected with.
I’m leaving it as a no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've gotta no-vote for the same reason as this. I'm honestly kind of iffy on Disney-being-creepy stories - maybe it's because I've heard a lot of "DISNEY IS BAD AND/OR FOR CHILDREN" growing up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I updated the requirements a bit. While I don't believe that the an anomaly needs to have super restrictive parameters for activating, I do agree that there would be too many people getting infected for the Foundation to even consider letting Disney keep the ride in operation. As such, I added another small condition that felt natural. I understand not liking the feel of the article, not much I can do about that though. Thanks for your input!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this article resembles a line of events that would happen in a Disney creepypasta— but the way that it's relegated into a repeated process that affects certain individuals takes away from it, as well as just being kinda cheesy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for pointing out the typo'd D-class number. Just fixed that.

As for why the ride is kept open, I'm planning on expanding on that with an exploration log or tale, whichever ends up being more compelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interview log randomly switches between "D-9258" and "D-9528".

Also, personally, the reasoning behind leaving the ride open bugged me. I'd feel better about it if they were prevented from shutting it down by some anomalous means, or if the anomaly reacted badly to being closed and so they decided it was less dangerous to keep it running with added safeguards.

Aside from those minor complaints, it's a somewhat bland Disney-based thing-that-uses-you-to-reproduce, but for some reason I really enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not super sold on this one, I think it's more personal gripes than any real criticism though. Disney being influential over the Foundation irked me slightly, idea is still neat though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree. On the other hand I appreciate the ending note tremendously enough for the worldbuilding that I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3387's anomalous effects manifest when an individual who has traveled to at least three other countries outside their nation of birth embarks on the ride for the third time.

Those aren't terribly unusual conditions to meet. I meet those conditions, as do a large number of other people who visit WDW.

Even beyond that, this feels too Standard Disney Creepypasta for my taste. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. It draws on the inherent creepiness of IASW, and doesn't bog down in excessive gore.

item.title: A small beginning
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone, this is my first ever published article on the site.

Thank you to A Random Day for creating SCP-3640, which heavily inspired me.

Thank you to taylor_itkin, Trilby, AshGray, Amarthon, marekv, and Illyrias_Acolyte for critiquing this piece.

The image is licensed under creative commons from

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:SmallWorldIndia.PNG

I hope you all enjoy this <3
